steps.title: Introduce the writer broadly
steps.content: An audience unfamiliar with Clara Hopkins needs more than one publication fact. The notes support an introduction that identifies her as an African American writer, presents the breadth of her genre-defining career, and gives Hagar's Daughter as a landmark example.

steps.title: Reject one-work summaries
steps.content: Choice A gives only authorship and dates for one novel. Choice C foregrounds publication details for the same novel, and Choice D names the novel's significance without introducing Hopkins or her larger career. The remaining choice gives identity, career scope, and a notable work. The answer is B.
